content.sentence: Tôi ___ đi đến trường vào buổi sáng.

translation.en: I usually go to school in the morning.

notes_md:
**thường thường** = *usually*, *most of the time*. It talks about habits or things that happen regularly.

It’s an adverb, so it goes before the main verb. It’s basically a softer, more casual version of **thường**. In modern speech, many people just say **thường**; **thường xuyên** is a bit stronger (“frequently”).


To remember: **thường** = usual → doubled = “yeah, pretty usual.”
notes:
thường thường = usually, most of the time. It talks about habits or things that happen regularly.

It’s an adverb, so it goes before the main verb. It’s basically a softer, more casual version of thường. In modern speech, many people just say thường; thường xuyên is a bit stronger (“frequently”).

To remember: thường = usual → doubled = “yeah, pretty usual.”
exampleSentences: Anh thường thường uống cà phê vào buổi sáng trước khi đi làm.
Chị thường thường đi bộ trong công viên mỗi cuối tuần.
Em thường thường ăn cơm với gia đình vào bữa tối.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I usually drink coffee in the morning before going to work.
I usually walk in the park every weekend.
I usually eat dinner with my family.

translation.en: I usually go to school in the morning.

content.sentence: Tôi ___ đi đến trường vào buổi sáng.

example.vi: Tôi thường thường đi bộ đến trường.
